critic_raw:
Issue SKIN-1 — Camera-Site-Label Collinearity
Camera model, site identity, and malignancy label are jointly correlated. The academic melanoma center uses older cameras and contributes most positives; suburban benign-dominant clinics use newer polarized cameras. Color augmentation and resolution normalization do not break this correlation — they reduce within-image variance, not between-site systematic differences. Evidence test: train model with site ID excluded; evaluate on held-out academic center cases using only suburban clinic camera conditions — if AUC drops substantially, the model learned camera signature.

Issue SKIN-2 — Referral Mix Shift at Academic Center
Biopsy triage clinic expansion during rollout shifts positive base rate at the site already contributing most melanomas. Not analyzed.

Issue SKIN-3 — No Site-Stratified Evaluation
All evaluation is pooled. Without site stratification, Issues 1 and 2 cannot be assessed from the reported data. Generalization claim is not supported.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
SKIN-1: The three-way collinearity is explicitly present in the data description. The augmentation argument applies to within-image variation; the critic is correct that it does not break the between-camera systematic correlation. I cannot defend the augmentation claim as resolving this confound.
SKIN-2: The rollout expansion is mid-study and at the primary melanoma site. Not analyzed. Concede.
SKIN-3: No site-stratified results. Concede.

Pass 2: All three conceded. Verdict: critique_wins.

debate_rounds.note: All points conceded at first defense pass. No open points for debate.

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — hidden_confounding_skin_sites_205 — Multi-Round Run 1

All three issues resolved at first pass. Defender had critic output available and correctly identified all three concerns as valid without rebuttal. Verdict: CRITIQUE_WINS
